title: On this day in history, Jan. 9, 2007, Steve Jobs introduces Apple iPhone at Macworld in San Francisco | New York Post
content: # On this day in history, Jan. 9, 2007, Steve Jobs introduces Apple iPhone at Macworld in San Francisco. Then-Apple CEO Steve Jobs holds the iPhone in San Francisco, California, on Jan. 9, 2007. Apple CEO Steve Jobs gave the world its first look at the iPhone — as well as a glimpse into a radically different future of personal computing and communications — on this day in history, Jan. 9, 2007. “It’s not just the bestselling gadget ever created: It’s probably the most influential one, too,” Wired wrote in a 2018 retrospective of the first decade of the iPhone. The Apple co-founder noted that the Macintosh in 1984 “changed the whole computer industry” and that the iPod (introduced on the same Jan. 9 date as the iPhone, but in 2001) “changed the entire music industry.”. The iPhone, and the advances in technology it forced on other smartphones, has had a profound impact on the way people live.

title: History of the iPhone - Wikipedia
content: # History of the iPhone. The **history of the iPhone** by Apple Inc. began in the early 2000s. The first iPhone "IPhone (1st generation)") was unveiled at Macworld 2007 and released later that year. However, Jobs noted in his announcement keynote for the first iPhone that he had been looking forward to it for two and a half years, suggesting that the project began during the summer of 2004. In 2002, after the iPod launched, Jobs realized that the overlap of mobile phones and music players would force Apple to get into the mobile phone business. Feeling that having to compromise with a non-Apple designer (Motorola) prevented Apple from designing the phone they wanted to make, Apple discontinued support for the ROKR in September 2006, and, after creating a deal with AT&T (at the time still called Cingular), released a version of iTunes that included references to an as-yet unknown mobile phone that could display pictures and video.
